name: plotnine (ggplot2 for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Python implementation of R's ggplot2 grammar

performance.notes: Static plots; similar performance to matplotlib; rendering time depends on complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Python implementation of R's ggplot2 Grammar of Graphics. Layered plot construction: data + aesthetics + geometries + statistics + scales + facets + themes. Familiar API for R users transitioning to Python. Works with pandas DataFrames. Supports faceted plots, statistical summaries, custom themes. Used in data science, statistics, academic publications. Better for statistical graphics than matplotlib; less interactive than plotly.

gotchas: Slower than matplotlib for complex plots
Python syntax differs from R ggplot2 in subtle ways
best_when: R users transitioning to Python wanting familiar ggplot2 API
Statistical graphics with Grammar of Graphics principles
summary: plotnine brings R's ggplot2 Grammar of Graphics to Python. Layered plot construction (data + aesthetics + geoms + stats + facets). Works with pandas DataFrames. Best for R users transitioning to Python or anyone preferring the Grammar of Graphics approach for statistical visualization. Less interactive than plotly; not as fast as matplotlib — but produces beautiful statistical graphics.
